# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-deployment
  labels:
    app: node
spec: 
  replicas: 3 # How many nodes will run this deployment? (replicas?)
  template: 
    metadata:
      labels: 
        app: node
    spec: 
      containers:
        - image
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: api 
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5000
      volumesMounts: 
      - mountPath: /usr/share/mongo-tools
        name: mongo-tools
---
apiVersion: v1 # The type of the resource we want to create. (apiVersion?)
kind: Service # What kind of resource are we defining? (kind?)
metadata:
  name: nodejs-svc # A unique name that identifies our resource. (metadata)
  labels:
    app: node # This is where we place some tags so we can filter down to certain parts of our resource tree later. (labels - kind of like an ID for the resource).
spec: 
  selector: 
    app: node
  ports: 
    - port: 80
      targetPort: api 
      protocol: TCP # Which protocol are we communicating on? (ports?)
